items.summary: South Australia has won back-to-back Sheffield Shield titles with a brilliant comeback victory over Victoria in the final at Melbourne's Junction Oval.

items.title: Dezi Freeman's path from an 'unemployable' sovereign citizen to Porepunkah police shooter
items.summary: Before Dezi Freeman's death brought an end to a long manhunt, his friends say he was a troubled and divisive figure who was drawn to the anti-authority "sovereign citizen" movement.

items.title: Second young Indigenous man dies in custody in Darwin within a week
items.summary: In less than a week, another Indigenous man in his 20s has died in custody in Darwin. The incidents were not related, but the cause of death for both men remains unknown.

items.title: Dezi Freeman killed by police in Victoria's north-east after seven-month hunt
items.summary: Freeman had been wanted since last August over the fatal shooting of two police officers at Porepunkah.

items.title: Cameron Green unable to bowl in IPL opener, Ajinkya Rahane calls out Cricket Australia
items.summary: After becoming the most expensive overseas purchase in IPL history, Australian Cameron Green is apparently not allowed to bowl in Kolkata's opening IPL match.

items.title: Nepal's former prime minister arrested over deaths during protests
items.summary: Nepalese police arrests former prime minister Khadga Prasad Oli as they investigate whether he was negligent in failing to prevent dozens of deaths during a crackdown on anti-corruption protests last September.

items.title: Tottenham Hotspur parts ways with Igor Tudor as disastrous EPL season continues following Ange Postecoglou sacking
items.summary: Tottenham announces it has parted ways with interim manager Igor Tudor as the EPL giant fights to avoid relegation.

items.title: Festival encourages people to see seaweed's 'importance and beauty'
items.summary: South Australia's rugged south-east coast is known for its cold water smashing into limestone cliffs. But beneath the surface is a global hotspot of seaweed biodiversity.

items.title: Hand-raised baby corals bring hope of restoring Great Barrier Reef
items.summary: Tens of thousands of baby corals, grown on land or settled from wild spawn slicks, have been transplanted onto the Great Barrier Reef.

items.title: WA tourist towns grapple with wildlife loss, damage to businesses after Cyclone Narelle
items.summary: In the popular tourist destination, it appears the category four cyclone has chewed up and spat out whole sections of the Ningaloo Reef onto dry land.

items.title: Farmers, fishers and remote Indigenous communities appeal for fuel price relief and supply solutions
items.summary: Farmers, fishers and business leaders are appealing to the federal and state governments to provide targeted relief to help them deal with massive fuel price increases caused by the Iran war, and the looming prospect of shortages.

items.title: Electric trucks trade diesel for batteries to make their mark on Australian roads
items.summary: A Central Coast business is converting diesel trucks to electric, amid rising fuel prices across Australia, with the first end-to-end electric delivery from Sydney to Canberra highlighting the potential of battery-powered freight.

items.title: Sam Kerr and Ellie Carpenter return to spark Chelsea Women's Super League win over Aston Villa
items.summary: Sam Kerr scores and sets up another goal with the help of Matildas teammate Ellie Carpenter as the Australians return to London to help Chelsea take down Aston Villa.

items.title: Rural families consider sacrificing children's sport, school runs as fuel costs soar
items.summary: Rural and remote families who are spending as much as $500 a week on fuel amid the current oil crisis say they are preparing to make tough decisions about whether to make school runs and to go without sport and even health care.

items.title: Food charities appeal for federal government help as fuel prices burden volunteers and budgets
items.summary: Charities supplying food relief to struggling Australians are grappling with the twin pressures of rising demand for help while their own fuel bills soar.

items.title: Trump threatens to seize Kharg Island, warns Iran to open Strait of Hormuz — as it happened
items.summary: The US president wrote on a social media post that "great progress is being made" in talks with Iran but said that if a deal is not reached, the US would broaden its offensive.

items.title: Kylie Minogue named AFL grand final pre-game entertainment
items.summary: Pop superstar Kylie Minogue has been named as the men's AFL grand final pre-match entertainment, the first time since 2021 that an Australian artist has headlined the event.

items.title: Yerrabi Yurwang Health Hub closing due to lack of funding, leaving First Nations patients in Canberra in the lurch
items.summary: An absence of funding from the ACT and federal governments is forcing the Yerrabi Yurwang Health Hub to close this week, affecting about 400 First Nations patients.

items.title: What you need to know about Iran today, with Matthew Doran
items.summary: The Israel Defense Forces has been forced to admit it edited a photo of Ali Shoeib to show him in a military uniform.

items.title: Local government politicians blurred during Brisbane City Council meeting livestream
items.summary: The incident sparked a debate over conspiracies, technology, TikTok, and clout-chasing in Australia's largest local council.

items.title: How to approach your employer about working from home and flexible work arrangements
items.summary: With rising fuel prices adding additional financial stress to household living costs, what are your rights when it comes to working from home — and what are the best ways to approach your employer about flexible work arrangements?

items.title: Adriana Rivas, the Bondi nanny accused of torture for Pinochet, makes last stand to evade extradition to Chile
items.summary: Adriana Rivas has been a fugitive in Australia since fleeing Chile while on bail 20 years ago. A federal court hearing will determine whether she is removed to face trial for the alleged crimes of torture and kidnapping, committed on behalf of the Pinochet regime.

items.title: Health insurers are expanding into GP services. Here's why it could be a threat to Medicare
items.summary: Major insurers like Medibank Private and Bupa are buying up GP clinics in a move that could lead to US-style managed care.

items.title: Australian fears about Iran war, fuel shortages run deeper than an anti-Trump attitude
items.summary: The pandemic demonstrated that when global events become local, Australians are quick to turn on even a mighty trading partner. There is a real capacity here for the war to sour our attitude to the US alliance.

items.title: Easter trip plans cancelled over petrol price hikes driven by Middle East war
items.summary: Rising fuel prices driven by the Middle East conflict are hitting the domestic tourism sector, with cancellations surging ahead of the Easter long weekend.

items.title: How Australia became hostage to fuel imports
items.summary: The early 2010s were the years of peak globalisation, when it seemed all trade barriers were on a one-way trip to elimination. That was before Donald Trump arrived and ruptured the global order.

items.title: From Australian Idol to The Piano, Guy Sebastian has tried most types of reality TV
items.summary: Singing his way into the Australian public's hearts way back in 2003, Guy Sebastian has since had a fascinating and varied career. We catch up with the star to find out about the little things that keep him going — from his two sons to his favourite TV show.

items.title: You, Me & Tuscany starring Halle Bailey and Regé-Jean Page marks return of the romcom
items.summary: You, Me & Tuscany is highly anticipated by fans of Halle Bailey and Regé-Jean Page, who can't wait for a rare romantic comedy to hit cinemas.

items.title: This week in history quiz: Are you ready to go back in time?
items.summary: Cast your mind back and see if you can conquer this week's round of trivia.

items.title: Adelaide 'deployment exercise' tests emergency services with details drawn from recent events
items.summary: South Australian emergency services conduct their largest mass casuality exercise in history in a bid to test coordination between agencies.

items.title: Australia to support NASA's Artemis II as first crewed Moon mission in 50 years launches
items.summary: Canberra-based communications systems will support NASA's first crewed lunar mission in more than half a century, helping astronauts orbit the Moon and return data.

items.title: Kangaroos' Tristan Xerri sent to AFL tribunal after alleged blood-wiping incident
items.summary: North Melbourne ruckman Tristan Xerri is sent directly to the AFL tribunal on a charge of serious misconduct after he allegedly wiped blood on Essendon captain Andy McGrath's face.

items.title: 'Gate of Tears' at risk of becoming second major oil bottleneck
items.summary: The opening of a new front in the Middle East war has put another vital shipping route on edge. Known in Arabic as the "Gate of Tears", analysts warn this narrow waterway could soon live up to its name.

items.title: Shane Flanagan on the challenge of coaching on after son's scary neck injury
items.summary: St George Illawarra five-eighth Kyle Flanagan was transported to hospital after a head knock in the Dragons' 22-14 loss to the Gold Coast Titans.

items.title: Free public transport ruled out for NSW commuters as Liberals, union renew calls for cost-of-living relief
items.summary: The NSW Liberals and the Rail, Tram and Bus Union (RTBU) have renewed calls for free public transport in response to surging fuel prices.

items.title: Labor's Toby Priest defeats Liberals' Stephen Patterson to win Morphett in SA election
items.summary: Liberal frontbencher Stephen Patterson loses his bid to be re-elected in the beachside seat of Morphett in the South Australian state election.

items.summary: After cancelling a full Sri Lankan wedding and a string of "disastrous" relationships, Sashi Perera found herself opposite a man who made believing in him simple.

items.title: Bullara Station feels 'intense' full force of category four Tropical Cyclone Narelle near Exmouth
items.summary: An outback cattle station and tourist ranch in WA's north has felt the full force of Cyclone Narelle, with vision emerging of the trail of destruction left by the powerful wind and heavy rain.

items.title: Demons overwhelm Blues in late MCG surge
items.summary: The Demons complete an amazing comeback and pile pressure on Carlton coach Michael Voss, while West Coast celebrates an inspirational victory on the road against Port Adelaide.

items.title: A secret stash and history mystery solved — why Birrundudu drawings are rewriting timeline of Aboriginal art
items.summary: A stash of more than 800 mysterious drawings has been revealed for the first time, rewriting the timeline of Aboriginal art and reconnecting families with long-lost relatives.

items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: 'No Kings' protests against Donald Trump draw crowds across US and Europe
items.summary: Huge crowds rally against US President Donald Trump from coast to coast in what organisers hope will be the largest single-day protest in US history.

items.title: Israeli military releases vision of strike on Lebanese journalists
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Andrew Hastie says Iran war a 'huge miscalculation' by Donald Trump
items.summary: Coalition frontbencher Andrew Hastie has warned the credibility of the United States is being damaged and says Australians may be losing faith in the alliance.

items.title: South Australia claims late wickets against Victoria on day four of Sheffield Shield final
items.summary: South Australia strikes twice late on day four to snatch the momentum from Victoria in the Sheffield Shield final at Melbourne's Junction Oval.

items.title: Why the 'perfect pair' of jeans remains elusive
items.summary: The enduring challenge of finding the "perfect pair" of jeans highlights the garment's cultural significance, and the opportunity to move toward more sustainable and more thoughtfully designed denim.

items.title: Scientists 'crack the code' as endangered palm cockatoo chick hatches in artificial nest
items.summary: Scientists rejoice as adult birds enter an artificial hollow to feed and incubate the egg, before hatching and eventually flying away.

items.title: Kyra Cooney-Cross to miss rest of season, Steph Catley and Caitlin Foord fire for Arsenal in WSL derby thrashing
items.summary: Kyra Cooney-Cross won't play for Arsenal again this season as she stays in Australia to be with her sick mum, but Caitlin Foord is back firing for the Gunners.

items.title: Houthis open up new front in Middle East war, making things much more complex for Trump
items.summary: One month into the Middle East conflict, the fighting has expanded again with another new front opening up as the Houthis launched their first attack. It creates further complexity for Donald Trump who now has to contain a widening conflict, writes Allyson Horn.

items.title: 15-year-old Tasmanian cricketer Mia Barwick set to ride wave of women's sporting boom
items.summary: Mia Barwick is just 15 and a multi-sport prodigy, bound for cricketing glory. She's part of the next generation of female sporting stars — but with that comes pressure of the spotlight.

items.title: Magistrate accuses ACT police and prosecutors of 'egregious conduct' over botched prosecution
items.summary: Lawyers for a Canberra man cleared of 14 family violence charges are calling for an apology and compensation, after police found evidence the complainant fabricated some claims.

items.title: Brisbane couple give up city life, careers, and move to outback to run Silverton Hotel
items.summary: Burnt out from city life and careers in Brisbane, Liz Murphy and Norm Matau knew they couldn't keep going. So they left it all behind and started a new life as outback publicans.

items.title: State library showcases photo archive of trailblazing Indigenous journalist Wayne Coolwell
items.summary: Journalist and broadcaster Wayne Coolwell documented years of Indigenous culture with his 35mm camera. The photographs are going on showcase at the State Library.

items.title: Showbags a highlight of sideshow alley for generations of Australians
items.summary: The "uniquely Australian" tradition of the showbag dates back more than a century, and shows no sign of dying out.

items.title: Indigenous families in NSW to receive more support to prevent child removals in $350m funding boost
items.summary: The team of case workers at Tharawal Aboriginal Corporation in south-west Sydney helping to prevent children from going into foster care is about to double in size.

items.title: Cyclone Narelle destroys WA banana crops, Carnarvon growers hit hard
items.summary: Fruit and vegetable producers in one of Western Australia's key food growing regions are counting the cost of Cyclone Narelle, with one banana grower reporting more than 80 per cent of his crop has been destroyed.

items.title: Regional WA businesses say it's a matter of weeks before fuel prices start hitting consumers
items.summary: Country business owners say they have worked to absorb price increases, but will be forced to pass on costs to consumers if the supply of fuel to regional WA isn't stabilised.

items.title: Locations of two new shark listening stations for Sydney Harbour revealed
items.summary: Sydney's lower north shore and eastern suburbs are likely locations for two new shark listening devices following a spate of attacks earlier this year.

items.title: Primary producers sell assets, consider lay-offs as fuel costs hit hard
items.summary: The sudden rise in fuel costs is forcing farmers to make difficult decisions, such as laying off workers and selling equipment to keep their businesses running.

items.title: Senegal defies Confederation of African Football, parading disputed Africa Cup of Nations trophy in friendly victory in Paris
items.summary: Senegal defies the decision to strip it of the Africa Cup of Nations trophy by parading it around the pitch before a 2-0 victory over Peru at the Stade de France in Paris.

items.title: AFL Victoria lawyers ask former footballer Daniel Hayes how much he drank before he was allegedly raped
items.summary: Daniel Hayes was 17 years old when he was allegedly raped by his coach while playing in an Australian Rules football talent league in Victoria.
